pi_items.source: Confirmed via environment setup scripts (task_utils.sh and setup_hec_ras.sh) which mount and copy the Muncie project to this directory.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which explicitly creates this directory for outputs.
Confirmed via export_result.sh which reads and validates the agent's output at this exact path.
Confirmed via export_result.sh which checks for the existence of this summary file.
Confirmed via the ground truth verification python script inside export_result.sh which calculates the hazard category using identical hardcoded logic (dv < 4: 'Low', < 12: 'Medium', < 25: 'High').
Confirmed via the python verifier in export_result.sh which asserts the presence of these exact normalized columns.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on the HEC-RAS 'Muncie' example project, which is based on real geospatial data for the White River. Setup scripts confirm the project is located at /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_projects/Muncie. The verification script strictly evaluates the agent's output CSV at /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/flood_hazard_index.csv, asserting the presence of specific columns (river_station, depth_ft, velocity_fps, dv_product, hazard_category). It computes the ground truth Depth-Velocity (DV) product internally and classifies hazards using exact thresholds: DV < 4 (Low), 4-12 (Medium), 12-25 (High), and > 25 (Extreme).
